title: CAC invalid, revoked, or expired
summary: Your Common Access Card (CAC) is invalid, and may be revoked or expired.
fix: Try again the next business day in case it's a transient revocation-list sync issue. If it persists, visit your nearest ID card / RAPIDS office to have the card re-issued. Bring two forms of ID.

title: CAC certificates revoked
summary: Your CAC certificates are invalid and access is revoked.
fix: Visit an ID card office for a new CAC. As a workaround, some users have reported success with a 32-bit Internet Explorer profile, but obtaining a new card is the correct fix.

title: ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR (Chrome)
summary: Error 107 (net::ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR): Unknown error.
fix: Re-run the Windows / Internet Options configuration (Trusted Sites, TLS 1.2, intermediate cert install). Re-import the DoD root + intermediate certificates from the DISA bundle.

title: ERR_BAD_SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CERT
summary: Error 117 (net::ERR_BAD_SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_CERT): Bad SSL client authentication certificate.
fix: First check your system clock — must be within 5 minutes of actual time and correct timezone. Then re-run the InstallRoot tool to install/repair DoD certificates. If recent, try System Restore to before the issue.

title: ERR_SSL_CLIENT_AUTH_SIGNATURE_FAILED (Mac Chrome)
summary: Error 141 on Mac Chrome.
fix: On macOS, Chrome's client-cert handling can choke. Use Safari instead, or install Thursby PKard / Centrify ExpressCAC middleware. Make sure DoD certs are in System keychain set to 'Always Trust'.

title: ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS
summary: There were too many redirects.
fix: Verify the system clock and timezone are correct. Clear cookies for the affected .mil site. Try a different browser to isolate.

title: HTTP 400 Bad Request after PIN
summary: HTTP 400 Bad Request after entering CAC PIN on Navy OWA.
fix: Usually transient. Wait 15 minutes and retry. If persistent, call the Navy 311 help desk at 855-NAVY-311 (855-628-9311).

title: SSL client certificate required
summary: Error 403.7 - Forbidden: SSL client certificate is required.
fix: Your browser isn't presenting a client certificate. Re-install DoD certificates with InstallRoot (DISA), insert your CAC, and re-launch the browser. On Edge/Chrome you may need to clear SSL state.

title: Internal Server Error / certificate revoked
summary: Error 500 or 'Certificate is revoked'.
fix: (1) Run the DoD Cross Cert Removal Tool (latest, v1.14+). (2) When prompted for a certificate, pick the right one — pick the cert that matches the site (Email vs Identity vs Authentication). (3) Close all browsers, then retry. If on Army systems, call AESD: 866-335-2769.

title: Network error reading installer file
summary: Error 1316. A network error occurred while attempting to read from the file C:\…
fix: Use the Microsoft Program Install and Uninstall troubleshooter. If installing ActivClient, copy the installer to your local drive first (not from a network share). As a last resort, create a fresh Windows user profile and install from there.

title: Installer insufficient privileges
summary: Error 1321: the installer has insufficient privileges to modify the file…
fix: Run the installer as Administrator (right-click → Run as administrator). Make sure your account has Admin rights, or log in with one that does.

title: Invalid drive X:
summary: Error 1327. Invalid Drive X:
fix: Open regedit and navigate to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\User Shell Folders. Replace any entries pointing to a missing drive letter with C:\Users\<you>\…  Reboot and retry.

title: Another installation in progress
summary: Error 1500, another installation in progress.
fix: Restart the computer first. If still failing, open Services.msc, find 'Windows Installer', stop it, then retry the install.

title: Fatal error during installation
summary: Error 1603: Fatal error during installation.
fix: Run the installer as Administrator. Temporarily disable antivirus. Copy the installer to your local drive (not a network share or read-only USB) and re-run.

title: Windows installer service could not be accessed
summary: Error 1719.
fix: Open an elevated Command Prompt: 'msiexec /unregister' then 'msiexec /regserver'. Reboot. If still broken, repair the Windows Installer service with sfc /scannow.

title: Problem with Windows Installer package
summary: Error 1722 on Vista/7 64-bit.
fix: Update Windows. Temporarily disable antivirus. Right-click installer → Run as administrator. As a last resort, create a new Windows user profile and install there.

title: Smart card service failed to start
summary: Error 1920 service smartcard failed to start (Windows 8+).
fix: Open Services.msc, find 'Smart Card', set Startup type to Automatic, then click Start. Also start 'Smart Card Device Enumeration Service'. Reboot.

title: Could not access VBScript runtime
summary: Error 2738. Could not access VBScript run time for custom action.
fix: Open elevated Command Prompt and run: 'regsvr32 vbscript.dll'. If that errors, also try 'regsvr32 jscript.dll'. Then retry the installer.

title: Server returned unexpected error 110
summary: Error 2755: attempting to install ActivClient CAC.
fix: Copy the installer to an unencrypted USB drive formatted FAT32 (not exFAT/NTFS encrypted), then run from there. Some encrypted filesystems break MSI custom actions.

title: CAC not found in reader (PIV activation)
summary: CAC NOT Found in Reader during PIV activation.
fix: Make sure the CAC is fully inserted (chip first, gold contact up). Open Device Manager → Smart card readers — your reader should appear. If using ActivClient, set it to expose PIV: ActivClient User Console → Tools → Advanced → 'Prefer GSC-IS over PIV EndPoint' = No.

title: Timeout (PIV activation)
summary: Time Out / Timing Out during PIV activation.
fix: DMDC RAPIDS / IDCO portal is overloaded — try again later. Workaround: activate PIV via ActivClient locally instead of the web portal.

title: Middleware not installed
summary: Middleware is either improperly installed or not installed at all.
fix: Install the latest ActivClient (or HID ActivID ActivClient). Reboot. Verify Smart Card services are running. Try PIV activation again.

title: PIV EndPoint interface (PIV activation)
summary: ActivClient may be configured to use the PIV EndPoint interface.
fix: ActivClient User Console → Tools → Advanced → Configuration → set 'Prefer GSC-IS over PIV EndPoint' from No to Yes. Restart the computer.

title: Webmail error (Mac/Windows)
summary: Error when accessing webmail (.mail.mil etc.).
fix: macOS: open Keychain Access, delete any entries for mail.mil and reinstall DoD certificates. Windows: open certmgr.msc, delete cached DoD certs, reinstall using InstallRoot.

title: Adding DoD certs to Mac
summary: Error when adding DoD certificates to macOS.
fix: Boot to Recovery and run Disk Utility → First Aid on your system disk. Then in Finder go to ~/Library/Keychains, move the folder to your Desktop, reboot, and reinstall DoD certs using the macOS All Certs Bundle (.pkg).

title: Adobe — Key does not exist
summary: When signing a PDF: 'The Windows Cryptographic Service Provider reported an error: Key does not exist.'
fix: Adobe Reader DC sometimes can't see the CAC private key. Open Adobe Reader → Edit → Preferences → Signatures → Identities & Trusted Certificates → set the default signing handler to 'Windows Certificate Store' (not 'Adobe Default Security'). If that fails, uninstall Reader DC and install Reader XI.

title: Adobe — Keyset not defined
summary: When signing a PDF: 'The keyset is not defined.'
fix: You're picking the wrong certificate. Try a different one — for signing PDFs, choose the DOD ID (Identity) or DOD SIGNATURE cert, not the EMAIL cert.
